exemplar.title: 11. Â¿Puede un conocimiento nuevo cambiar las creencias o los valores establecidos?

detailedExemplarData.title: 11. Â¿Puede un conocimiento nuevo cambiar las creencias o los valores establecidos?

strengths: Three specific realâworld objects are clearly identified, each with a stated context, fulfilling the requirement for concrete artefacts.
The commentary consistently links each object to IA prompt 11, demonstrating how new knowledge can reinforce, transform or be resisted by existing beliefs and values.
Appropriate references are provided (for example, for the Copernicus sketch), showing an attempt to ground claims in external sources.
Structural coherence is strong: each object is clearly introduced with headers and maintains consistency across the exhibition.
The student clearly states the chosen prompt and optional theme up front, defining the scope and focus of the exhibition.
weaknesses: Explanations remain largely descriptive and lack precise engagement with core TOK concepts (e.g., authority, justification, paradigms, language).
Analytical depth is inconsistent: assertions about how knowledge âmouldsâ or âcomplementsâ beliefs are not supported by detailed evidence or TOK terminology.
There is noticeable repetition across objects, with similar claims about context-dependence of acceptance rather than distinct analyses per artefact.
Justifications for Object 2 and Object 3 are incomplete or cut off, limiting the clarity and completeness of the argument.
The conclusion fails to synthesise insights across the three objects, merely restating general points without drawing out distinct lessons.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band: three specific objects are identified with contexts and linked to the IA prompt, and many points are supported by evidence. However, the analysis is often superficial, overly descriptive, and occasionally incomplete. Strengths in structure, sourcing and linking justify placing this work at the upper end of the band. Assigning 6 marks reflects its solid fulfillment of mid-level criteria while acknowledging the need for deeper TOK engagement and more precise, analytical justification.

annotations.comment: The section header for Object 1 is clear and immediately identifies the artefact, aiding reader orientation within the exhibition structure.
The inclusion of a real-world certificate image grounds the discussion in a specific context, satisfying the requirement to use concrete artefacts rather than generic examples.

The description of the ESO diploma focuses on personal experience without linking explicitly to how this knowledge challenges or transforms existing beliefs; the student should connect to TOK ideas such as authority and justification.

Using Copernicusâs heliocentric diagram directly links to historical shifts in knowledge, fulfilling the requirement for an artefact that illustrates resistance to or transformation of existing beliefs.

The studentâs justification of Object 2 remains at a descriptive level; to strengthen the analysis, they should explore the role of language in framing the heliocentric model and its epistemic implications.

References to Newtonian paradigm are mentioned but not integrated into the argument. The student should unpack how shifts in scientific language and methodology affected acceptance of the heliocentric view.
The section header for Object 2 identifies the source but does not specify its real-world context (e.g., publication date, audience); adding these details would sharpen the artefactâs relevance.
The historical account of Copernicus and Galileo demonstrates awareness of resistance by authority, a valuable link to how new knowledge can challenge established institutions.

The student asserts a âneutral positionâ without supporting evidence; they should cite specific outcomes or scholarly debates to justify why predisposition matters for acceptance of new knowledge.

This paragraph reiterates previous points about resistance but fails to engage with the promptâs focus on beliefs versus values; consider distinguishing how each is affected differently by new knowledge.
Object 3 is clearly introduced with the title and artefact type, maintaining the structural consistency across all three artefacts.
The image of the annotated text lacks a caption describing its provenance; include information on when and why the annotations were made to establish context and credibility.

The student uses the annotations metaphorically but does not link this explicitly to the theme of knowledge and language; incorporating terminology such as âsemantic frameworksâ would deepen the connection.
The student clearly states the chosen IA prompt and optional theme, providing a solid foundation for the exhibition by defining scope and context.

The introductory paragraph offers definitions but remains purely descriptive without engaging core TOK concepts such as justification or paradigm shifts; the student should integrate these to deepen analysis.
Including word count here is administrative. Future submissions should omit administrative details from the main commentary to maintain focus on TOK analysis.
This sentence is cut off and lacks completion. Ensure each justification is fully developed to articulate how the artefact exemplifies the prompt rather than ending abruptly.
The final explanation of Object 3 is cut off mid-sentence. Ensure full justifications are presented to demonstrate how the artefact relates to resisting or reinforcing existing values.

The concluding paragraph restates earlier points without synthesizing lessons from the three objects; a stronger conclusion would compare how each artefact uniquely illustrates transformation, reinforcement, or resistance.
The summary is too general and does not return to specific examples. The student should reference each object in turn to demonstrate varied outcomes of new knowledge on beliefs and values.
Well-formatted reference list follows an academic style. For cohesion, consider citing page numbers or direct quotations when referencing the Copernicus sources.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
